name: VARNAM
description: Voltage indicator using opsin-FRET architecture with red fluorescent protein for improved tissue penetration.

spectrum.name: VARNAM
spectrum.note: VARNAM is an eFRET sensor (Ace2N opsin + mRuby3); the fluorescence reporter is mRuby3. The displayed excitation/emission is the FPbase mRuby3 spectrum verbatim (ex 558 nm / em 592 nm), matching VARNAM's reported peaks.

kinetics.note: Amplitude-weighted mean τ of a biexponential fit (depolarizing/repolarizing voltage steps in HEK cells, voltage clamp, room temp 22-25 °C, n = 4). Rise: τfast 0.88 ± 0.13 ms, τslow 5.2 ± 0.5 ms → on = 3.04 ms. Decay: τfast 0.80 ± 0.44 ms, τslow 4.7 ± 0.3 ms → off = 2.75 ms. The paper reports only τfast/τslow (Table S1), NOT the component amplitudes, so a 50:50 fast:slow split is assumed; if the fast component actually dominates, the true mean is faster (toward the fast τ). An earlier duplicate entry labelled 34 °C was removed (no 34 °C kinetics in the paper).

dynamicRangeData.note: ΔF/F for the -70 → +30 mV (100 mV) step, read directly off the origin-paper steady-state F-V curve (Fig. 2b, VARNAM dark-red curve; the same curve stored in voltage.custom, which gives deltaF = -13.5% at +30 mV). CORRECTED from a prior -11.8%, which disagreed with the page's own digitized F-V reading of this same figure. The main text separately quotes -14.1 ± 0.4% for a 120 mV depolarization (-70 → +50 mV, SNR = 49, n = 14 cells) - the wider window, kept here only as context.

ΔF/F0 = -17.9 ± 0.6% (n = 6 cells) measured over a -70 → +40 mV (110 mV) step in HEK293T cells - NOT a 100 mV step. The paper's main text explicitly states this window ('When the membrane potential is altered stepwise from -70 to +40 mV, the whole-cell fluorescence of VARNAM ... change by -17.9 ± 0.6%'). Stored value is therefore over a slightly wider window than the 100 mV standard and is correspondingly a modest overstatement; the -70 → +30 value could not be read reliably off the crowded overlapping Fig. 1B curves, so the paper's stated 110 mV number is kept with this window noted. sourceFigure note corrected from the earlier (wrong) '-70 to +30 mV / 100 mV step' label.

sensitivityData.note: Mean optical ΔF/F per current-evoked single AP = -4.8 ± 0.3% (SNR = 12, n = 7 neurons) in mouse acute cortical slices; the 'AP size' bar in Fig. 3d (magnitude stored). sourceFigure corrected from 'Fig. 3b' (the example-trace/image panel) to 'Fig. 3d' (the quantified AP-size bar chart, VARNAM vs Ace-mRuby3 vs Ace-mNeon).

Origin-paper per-AP value: optical responses to current-induced APs had a mean spike amplitude of 8.4 ± 0.3% ΔF/F (SNR = 36, n = 8 neurons) in primary cortical neurons (1P widefield). Magnitude stored (VARNAM is negative-going; depolarizing AP decreases F). Stated in the main text alongside the current-induced AP traces in Fig. 2f.

brightnessData.note: Normalized resting fluorescence in HEK cells, normalized to the average resting intensity of Ace-mNeon (= 1.0; Ace-mNeon is the brightest of the panel and is the normalization reference, not plotted as a bar). VARNAM bar ≈ 0.71, statistically indistinguishable from Ace-mRuby3 (~0.69, P > 0.99) and far brighter than FlicR1/FlicR2 (~0.18). CORRECTED from a prior ratio of 1.0, which incorrectly treated VARNAM as the normalization anchor; the figure normalizes to Ace-mNeon, so VARNAM/Ace-mNeon ≈ 0.71.
VARNAM ~580 a.u. vs Cepheid1b ~960 a.u. per cell (Fig. S5)
VARNAM ~580 a.u. vs Cepheid1s ~250 a.u. per cell (Fig. S5); VARNAM (mRuby3) is ~2.3-fold brighter than the dimmer, more photostable Cepheid1s (mRuby4).

photostabilityData.note: Fraction remaining at 1 min under continuous 565 nm LED at 15 mW/mm² in HEK cells. Consistent with the photobleaching time constant τ = 256 s reported for VARNAM (Suppl. Fig. 7a): exp(-60/256) = 0.79.

Digitized F/F0 at 10 min ≈ 0.42 from the Fig. S11B photobleaching curve (right panel, dark-red VARNAM curve; 565 nm LED at 25 mW/mm², HEK cells). Corrected from a prior value of 63%, which had been normalized to the post-initial-drop plateau rather than the t=0 baseline; VARNAM is in fact the fastest-bleaching of the three red sensors. See the photobleach[] entry for the full digitized F/F0 curve.

apWidthData.sample: Drosophila, PPL1-α'2α2 dopaminergic neuron, in vivo
apWidthData.note: Optical single-AP FWHM of spontaneous spikes, mean 3.6 ± 0.1 ms (mean amplitude 0.85 ± 0.05% ΔF/F, n = 2,213 spikes), in a PPL1-α'2α2 dopaminergic neuron in live Drosophila; 1P widefield 550-nm epifluorescence, acquired at 500 Hz (2 ms/frame) with 2x2 binning. The measured width is partly frame-rate-limited (2 ms/frame is non-negligible vs 3.6 ms). Co-measured Ace-mNeon FWHM in the same cell type was 3.9 ± 0.2 ms (Suppl. Fig. 15a,b). Kannan et al. 2018 also plots a mouse acute-slice optical FWHM (~4.6 ms, Fig. 3d) at an unreported frame rate (400 Hz-5 kHz range).

photobleach.note: VARNAM (mRuby3 reporter) photobleaching digitized from Fig. S6A, where it was measured alongside Cepheid1 as a comparator (1.59 W/cm² = 15.9 mW/mm² 561 nm wide-field, HEK293T; Table S3 of the Cepheid paper lists VARNAM's FP as mRuby3, peaks 558/592). Red curve with a fast initial drop (≈1.0→0.8 in ~20 s) then a slow decline; bi-exponential fit, model-free t₇₅% ≈ 164 s. The primary subjects of this figure are Cepheid1b/Cepheid1s. (An earlier version of this note mislabeled the reporter as 'cpmApple'; VARNAM's fluorophore is mRuby3, an mRuby2 derivative.)

Origin-paper photobleaching (Fig. S11B, right/red panel, the dark-red VARNAM curve) in HEK cells under continuous 565 nm LED illumination at 25 mW/mm² (n=4 wells, ~100 cells/well). Digitized by HSV color separation, verified against the figure. Rapid initial drop (1.0→~0.63 in ~12 s) then slow decay, so the model-free t75% crossing lands inside the transient (~6 s) and is unrepresentative — the half-life t50 ≈ 368 s is stored instead. Biexponential fit; F/F0 at 10 min ≈ 0.42 (the lowest of the three red sensors). Companion curves in the same panel: VARNAM2, pAceR. (A second, lower-intensity recording exists from the Cepheid paper, Fig. S6A entry above.)

Cross-GEVI value from the HVI origin paper (Liu 2021, doi:10.1038/s41557-021-00641-1), whose primary subject is HVI; VARNAM was co-plotted. Digitized from the gray curve in Extended Data Fig. 4g of the HVI origin paper (Liu 2021), a representative photobleaching trace in HEK293T cells under continuous 1P 561 nm illumination at 4.8 W/cm² (= 48 mW/mm², mRuby3); F/F0 normalized to t=0, 20 Hz sampling. Model-free t75 ≈ 70 s (t50 ≈ 163 s), versus the cohort half-life 154 ± 7 s (n = 4) (Supplementary Table 6). Representative curve, so it may run slightly off the cohort mean. Cross-overlaid with the other sensors in the same panel.

Origin-paper photobleach curve (Kannan et al. 2018, the paper that introduced VARNAM). HEK cells, continuous 565-nm LED at 15 mW/mm², n = 9 cells; the panel is normalized to 100% at t = 0. Digitized the dark-red VARNAM curve (the lower of the two red curves; Ace-mRuby3 is the brighter/upper red, no page). Model-free t75 ≈ 66 s; biexponential fit τ1 = 5.5 s (13%) + τ2 = 452 s (87%). Consistent with the paper's stated single-exponential τ = 256 s (a τ = 256 s monoexponential alone implies t75 ≈ 74 s). Co-plotted with Ace-mNeon (505-nm LED, cross-overlaid here via the shared source + Fig. S7a) and Ace-mRuby3.
